alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years at Google Devices and Services with ownership of requirements, logic design, UAT, rollout, and transformation—but product-operations experience rather than native product design leadership
alignments.job_requirement: Lead end-to-end experience design and set world-class UX/UI standards for complex, data-rich products

alignments.candidate_evidence: Two promotions and scaling of global commercial operations teams at Google, but no direct evidence of building or managing a design team
alignments.job_requirement: Manage and develop senior and staff-level product designers across London and Singapore studios

alignments.candidate_evidence: Advanced Excel, Sheets, SQL, BigQuery; builds Looker dashboards; proficient with complex data queries and operational systems—analytical depth but not interaction or information-architecture design
alignments.job_requirement: Deep strengths in interaction design, information architecture, and UX for complex workflows and data-heavy products

alignments.candidate_evidence: Data-informed operations background; no direct evidence of user research, UX research methodology, or design validation practice
alignments.job_requirement: Familiar with partnering on user research and quantitative analysis to inform decisions and measure impact

gaps.gap: No native product design or UX/UI background; career is strategy, operations, and commercial transformation, not design craft or design leadership
gaps.mitigation: Candidate would require a fundamental career pivot. Design leadership at director level, managing senior designers, demands deep design craft credibility built over years of hands-on design work.

gaps.gap: No experience leading or managing product designers; Google experience is operations and commercial teams, not design teams
gaps.mitigation: Design team leadership and people development in design requires understanding of design critique, craft standards, and design mentorship—outside stated background.

gaps.gap: No demonstrated UX research, design validation, or discovery practice; background is business analytics and operational metrics
gaps.mitigation: User research, design experimentation, and qualitative design validation differ materially from operational reporting and quantitative analysis.

gaps.gap: No developer platform, API, SDK, or fintech/payments domain experience cited
gaps.mitigation: Domain and product context can be learned, but without design foundation, ramp would be steep and role-critical responsibilities would suffer.

output.level_rationale: Posted role requires 12+ years in product/UX design (design-specific) with 4+ years managing senior designers. Director title, multi-region studio leadership, and mandate to set design vision and standards across complex financial and developer products signal L6 scope. JD does not cite executive/enterprise breadth typical of L7+.
output.summary: Director of Product Design role is fundamentally a design-craft and design-leadership position requiring 12+ years of native product/UX design experience and 4+ years managing senior designers. Candidate's eight-year track record is strategy, operations, and commercial transformation—not product design, design teams, UX research, or interaction/information-architecture work. While candidate brings data fluency and complex-system thinking, the role's core requirements (design vision, design team leadership, design validation, design craft standards) are outside candidate's expertise domain. This is a category mismatch, not a gap-within-function.
uncertainties: Candidate's actual hands-on design experience or coursework in design/HCI is not detailed in profile
Airwallex company stage and design-team maturity not stated; context matters for whether director scope is truly L6 or early-stage-inflated
Candidate's broader portfolio or side design work not documented
